title: What is volatility in Java? How does the volatile keyword work?

answers.junior: volatile makes a variable visible to all threads. Changes are always seen immediately.
answers.middle: volatile ensures updates to a variable are visible to other threads. It prevents caching and guarantees read/write visibility.
answers.senior: volatile provides visibility, not atomicity. Use for simple flags. For compound actions, use synchronized or java.util.concurrent. Volatile variables are not locked, so race conditions can occur.
answers.tricky: Volatile is for visibility, not synchronization. Use Atomic classes for atomic operations. Analyze memory consistency effects. Prefer higher-level concurrency tools for complex scenarios.
